Elementwise.issues: dropout and all 3 rope variants (rope_neox, rope_non_neox, rope_llama31) are failing tests. 13 ops lack test coverage including where, clamp, masked_fill, nan_to_num, alibi, sinusoidal, and several activations. Benchmark data is entirely absent — the log shows 0 qualified/underperforming/failed across 61 benched ops, which suggests a reporting pipeline issue rather than actual missing benchmarks.
Elementwise.evaluation: Core elementwise and activation ops are in good shape functionally. Fix the 4 failing rope/dropout tests as a priority since these are critical for LLM inference. Investigate why benchmark results are not surfacing in the log despite 61 ops being marked as benched.

Reduce.issues: All 20 ops pass tests and all 20 are marked benched, but the benchmark log reports 0 in every bucket. This is a benchmark reporting pipeline anomaly — results exist but are not being captured.
Reduce.evaluation: Functionally complete and fully tested. The only action needed is diagnosing why benchmark results are not flowing into the log; this is likely a systemic reporting issue affecting multiple categories.

Norm.issues: qk_norm is not implemented (impl=N), leaving 1 op missing from both test and bench coverage. Benchmark log shows 0 results despite 9 ops being marked benched — same reporting anomaly as other categories.
Norm.evaluation: Near-complete category with strong test results. Implement qk_norm to close the gap. Resolve the benchmark reporting pipeline to confirm performance baselines are being met.

Conv & Pooling.issues: Zero implementation across all 16 operators. This entire category is unstarted.
Conv & Pooling.evaluation: Conv & Pooling is a blank slate. Given TileOPs is LLM-focused, convolutions are lower priority, but pooling ops may be needed for vision-language models. Recommend deferring this category and creating a formal backlog item with priority relative to roadmap needs.

GEMM.issues: Only 3 of 19 ops have tests (gemm_fp16, gemv_fp16, groupgemm_fp16) despite 9 being implemented. fp8 variants, small_batch, and bmm variants all lack test coverage. The entire lowbit_gemm and sparse_gemm subcategories are unimplemented. Benchmark log shows 0 results despite 11 ops marked benched.
GEMM.evaluation: GEMM is a critical category for LLM performance and has significant test coverage gaps. Add tests for all implemented fp8 and small_batch variants immediately. Prioritize w4a16 and w8a8 implementation as these are essential for quantized inference workloads.

Quantize.issues: Only fp8_per_tensor is implemented and tested. fp8_per_block and chain_speculative_sampling benchmarks are explicitly failing (results expected but not found in log). 9 of 10 ops are unimplemented including all int8 and int4 variants.
Quantize.evaluation: Quantization coverage is critically low for a production LLM library. The 2 bench failures (fp8_per_block, fp8_per_block) need immediate investigation as they indicate a regression or environment issue. Prioritize implementing int8_per_tensor and int8_per_channel as foundational quantization ops.

Sampling.issues: Only chain_speculative_sampling is implemented and tested, but its benchmark is explicitly failing. 6 of 7 ops are unimplemented including top_k, top_p, and temperature_scale which are essential for inference.
Sampling.evaluation: Sampling is severely underdeveloped. Fix the chain_speculative_sampling benchmark failure first. Then prioritize top_k, top_p, and temperature_scale as these are required for any practical LLM inference pipeline.

Flash Attention.issues: All 8 implemented ops pass tests cleanly. Missing ops include flash_prefill_varlen_bwd, flash_decode_varlen_fwd, flash_chunked_prefill_fwd, all MLA paged/prefill variants, nsa_decode_fwd, and both DSA variants. Benchmark log shows 0 results despite 8 ops marked benched.
Flash Attention.evaluation: Implemented Flash Attention ops are functionally solid. Next implementation priorities should be flash_chunked_prefill_fwd and mla_prefill_fwd given their importance for long-context and MLA-based models. Resolve benchmark reporting to validate performance against baselines.

MoE.issues: permute_align passes tests and benchmarks despite impl=N, which is a data inconsistency — either the impl flag is wrong or the test/bench mappings are pointing to an external implementation. All fused_moe variants (deepseek, glm, kimi, qwen) are unimplemented.
MoE.evaluation: MoE is largely unimplemented, which is a significant gap for modern MoE-based LLMs. Clarify the impl=N discrepancy for permute_align. Prioritize fused_moe_deepseek as the most widely deployed MoE architecture in the target use case.

Linear Attention.issues: gla_chunkwise and gla_recurrence pass tests and benchmarks despite impl=N — same inconsistency as permute_align in MoE. retnet_chunkwise and retnet_recurrence are fully missing. Benchmark log shows 0 results despite 6 ops marked benched.
Linear Attention.evaluation: Strong functional results across tested ops. Resolve the impl=N inconsistency for GLA variants. Implement retnet variants to complete the category, and fix benchmark reporting to surface the 6 existing benchmark results.

SSM.issues: Both mamba1 and mamba2 are unimplemented with no test or benchmark coverage.
SSM.evaluation: SSM is unstarted. Given Mamba's growing relevance in hybrid architectures, this should be scheduled. Mamba2 is the more architecturally current target and may be worth prioritizing over mamba1.

overall.summary: TileOPs has solid functional coverage in its mature categories — Reduce is fully complete, Norm and Flash Attention are near-complete, and Elementwise is mostly healthy with a few rope/dropout regressions to fix. However, a systemic benchmark reporting pipeline failure is masking all performance data across every category, making it impossible to validate perf baselines tonight. Critical LLM inference categories — Quantize, Sampling, MoE, and GEMM lowbit variants — remain severely underdeveloped and represent the largest gaps relative to production readiness.
recommendations: Investigate and fix the benchmark reporting pipeline immediately — all categories show 0 qualified/underperforming results despite 116 ops being marked benched, which means nightly perf regression detection is completely blind.
Prioritize implementing core inference primitives: top_k/top_p sampling, int8 quantization variants, and fused_moe_deepseek, as these are blockers for end-to-end LLM inference support.

Fix the 4 failing Elementwise tests (dropout, rope_neox, rope_non_neox, rope_llama31) and resolve the impl=N data inconsistencies for gla_chunkwise, gla_recurrence, and permute_align to keep metadata trustworthy for tracking.
